text: Standing just outside the glass, I sense anticipation—the threshold before me is a boundary between familiar sterility and the vibrant, almost alien glow of discovery. My own reflection is faint in the glass, as if challenging me to see past my own assumptions. When I step into the R+D space, bold colors press close, screens and text envelop me in their dialogue. I am both observed and observing, curious and displaced. This environment feels like a laboratory for both intellect and emotion—a reminder that to innovate or connect, one must first surrender comfort and become visible in new ways. I am aware of my vulnerability, and yet, also a sense of possibility.
